page.content: The analysis of the data from Table 1 reveals various studies on anesthesia techniques and patient demographics. Mahadevan, D (2012) involved 26 patients undergoing SNB and 26 patients undergoing LIA, with a gender distribution of 23 males and 29 females, a mean age of 67.2 years for SNB and 67.5 years for LIA, utilizing general anesthesia and standard medial parapatellar surgery, administering 20 mL of 0.375% levobupivacaine for both FNB and LIA, and PCA for 48 hours for pain management. Safa, B (2014) had 33 SNB and 32 LIA patients, with 32 males and 33 females, a mean age of 61.2 years for SNB and 60.7 years for LIA, used spinal anesthesia, and described no specified surgery method, also applying 20 mL of 0.5% ropivacaine for FNB and LIA, and 50 mL of 0.2% ropivacaine for concomitant pain management. Tanikawa, H (2014) reported 23 SNB and 23 LIA patients, with gender distribution of 7 males and 39 females, mean ages of 72 years and 71 years, respectively, under general anesthesia and standard mid-vastus approach, using 20 mL of 0.375% ropivacaine for FNB and LIA, with a complex pain regimen.

page.content: The data continues similarly for Gi, E (2014), Nagafuchi, M (2015), Uesugi, K (2014), and Spangehl, M. J (2015). Table 2 illustrates microRNA expression levels related to GVHD grades, showing miR-146a-5p and miR-155-5p expression with corresponding patient counts: ↑ for Grade 0 (8 patients) and Grade I–III (7 patients), ↓ for Grade 0 (5 patients) and Grade I–III (10 patients), ↓ for Grade 0 (7 patients) and ↑ for Grade I–III (5 patients), and ↑ for Grade 0 (6 patients) and ↓ for Grade I–III (6 patients).
